fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer audiologist license to georgia, audiologist compact georgia, georgia audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Professional Licensing Boards Division, Office of the Secretary of State

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series offered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Georgia.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account on the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and complete the online application for Audiologist licensure, indicating if you are applying by examination or endorsement.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all required documents, including official transcripts verifying a doctorate degree from a CAA-accredited program, proof of U.S. citizenship or legal alien status, and any other forms specified in the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) application.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Scores
steps.description: Request that official scores for the National Examination in Audiology (Praxis Exam) be sent directly to the Georgia State Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology from the testing agency.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Remit all required application fees, including the initial application fee, through the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS).

steps.title: Respond to Board Requests
steps.description: Monitor your application status within the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and be prepared to provide any additional information or documentation requested by the Board during the review process.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Georgia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Georgia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Georgia
hero.dek: Applicants for Audiologist licensure in Georgia must apply online through the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS).

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Georgia Online Licensing System (GALS) and click 'Register a Person' to create a User ID and password. Once registered, log in and select 'New Application' to begin the Audiologist licensure application.
